trade.summary: Significant bilateral trade, boosted by CETA. Canada exports crude oil, natural gas, aircraft, agricultural products, and precious metals to Italy. Italy exports machinery, vehicles, fashion, food, wine, and pharmaceuticals to Canada.

diplomatic.summary: Excellent relations rooted in the massive Italian-Canadian diaspora (1.5 million Canadians of Italian descent). Both are G7 members and NATO allies. Cultural, political, and economic ties are deep. Regular high-level visits and strong consular cooperation.

military.summary: Both are NATO allies with active defense cooperation. Joint training, operations (including Afghanistan, Libya), and equipment procurement cooperation. Italy's Leonardo has supplied aircraft simulators and systems to Canada.

financial.summary: Strong bilateral investment. Italian companies (Stellantis, ENI, UniCredit, Leonardo) have Canadian operations. Canadian pension funds invest in Italian infrastructure. Italian-Canadian business community is very active.

energy.summary: Canada exports crude oil and LNG to Italy. Italy has sought to diversify energy sources post-Ukraine war and Canada is among potential suppliers. ENI has Canadian upstream assets.

scientific.summary: Active scientific cooperation in physics (TRIUMF-INFN links), astronomy, marine science, and health. Large Italian-Canadian academic community. Joint research programs through national science funding agencies.
